title: Does the abelian group C_3 x C_207 admit a (621,156,39)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean could at most seal the conditional quotient-and-character obstruction. It does not bridge the missing unconditional multiplier input needed for the selected exact-row theorem, so formalization would not produce a human-ready packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for now. The target row remains micro-paper eligible in principle, but the surviving packet is only a conditional structural obstruction and no longer supplies the title theorem required by the one-shot micro-paper lane.
family_affinity: strong residual-row anchor in the abelian difference-set program; proof uses multiplier and quotient structure rather than search
generalization_signal: moderate; the prime-quotient orbit and low-order-character template may transfer to nearby rows once a genuine multiplier theorem is available
strongest_honest_claim: Assuming a valid 13-multiplier normalization for a hypothetical (621,156,39)-difference set in C_3 x C_207, the C_23 quotient fibers are forced to (2;5^11;9^11) up to orbit swap and the order-3 character argument on C_3 x C_9 then yields a contradiction; this is structural but does not settle the unconditional exact row.
paper_title_hint: A conditional orbit obstruction for the (621,156,39) row in C_3 x C_207 under a 13-multiplier hypothesis
candidate_theorem_slice: Conditional structural slice only: under a valid 13-multiplier normalization, the C_23 quotient profile is forced to (2;5^11;9^11) up to swap, after which the order-3 character congruence rules out that case.
publication_packet_quality: preserved structural conditional slice, but not a publication-ready exact-row packet
next_action: freeze this packet as a conditional VARIANT artifact and move it aside unless the same exact-row lane yields either an explicit source-backed unconditional multiplier theorem or a multiplier-free contradiction
